subject: [RFC PATCH 31/40] mm: page_alloc: per-(zone, order, mt) PASS_1 hint cache

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a hint cache to improve the performance of page allocations in the Linux kernel. The cache stores the most recently used superpage block (SPB) for each zone, order, and migratetype tuple, allowing the kernel to quickly retrieve the next SPB to allocate from. This reduces the number of iterations required during page allocation, resulting in a significant reduction in walk depth and an increase in hint hit rate. The patch also includes measurements showing a 99% hint hit rate and a median walk depth reduction from ~50 SPBs to ~5 SPBs on a test system with a steady workload.
